'''Luis Johnson''' (born 20 February 1999) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He has previously played for [[Warrington Wolves]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He has spent time on loan and dual registration at [[Rochdale Hornets]], [[Widnes Vikings]], [[Dewsbury Rams]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Johnson was born in [[Leeds]], [[West Yorkshire, England]] and is of [[England|English]] and [[Jamaica]]n descent.

He started his amateur career at [[Oulton Raiders]] in [[Leeds]] at 4 years of age and was selected by the [[Castleford Tigers]] at 15 years old to join their scholarship programme. He went on to their academy programme but, in 2018, the [[Warrington Wolves]] bought him out of his contract for £45,000 and Johnson signed a 3-year deal to join the club.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2017-12-08 |title=Warrington Wolves sign Castleford Tigers teenager Luis Johnson |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/11161962/warrington-wolves-sign-castleford-tigers-teenager-luis-johnson |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

==Career==
===Warrington Wolves===
In 2018, Johnson made his [[Super League]] début for the [[Warrington Wolves]] against the [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref name="Wigan Warriors v Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/ERLP4069366|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180805182924/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/ERLP4069366|url-status=dead|archive-date=August 5, 2018|title=Wigan Warriors v Warrington Wolves|publisher=bbc.co.uk|date=14 September 2018|accessdate=14 September 2018}}</ref>

Johnson spent time on dual registration with the [[Rochdale Hornets]], and on loan at the [[Widnes Vikings]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=2019-03-05 |title=Warrington youngster joins Widnes on loan |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-youngster-joins-widnes-on-loan |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=2019-06-17 |title=Hull KR sign Luis Johnson on loan |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-sign-luis-johnson-on-loan |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 10 December 2020, [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] announced that Johnson would join on a permanent basis, signing a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite news |title=Robbie Mulhern: Warrington sign Hull KR prop as Luis Johnson goes in opposite direction |work=BBC Sport |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55253311 |accessdate=13 Dec 2020}}</ref> This was part of a swap deal that would see [[Robbie Mulhern]] move the other way and join [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].

In June 2021, Johnson signed a two-year contract extension with Hull KR.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Byram |first=Richard |date=22 June 2021 |title=Hull KR forward Luis Johnson had "no hesitation" agreeing new contract with Robins |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-forward-luis-johnson-had-no-hesitation-agreeing-new-contract-with-robins-3282897 |access-date=24 November 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> In September 2021, Johnson played for Hull KR in their 28-10 semi-final loss against the [[Catalans Dragons]] as they fell one game short of the grand final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/sep/30/catalans-dragons-hull-kr-super-league-play-off-semi-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=Catalans Dragons blast past Hull KR and into their first Super League Grand Final|website=www.theguardian.com|date=30 September 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12415081/barrie-mcdermotts-2021-betfred-super-league-season-review/|title=Barrie McDermott's 2021 Betfred Super League season review|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/58663060.amp/|title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 0-19 Hull KR - Robins set up a semi-final at Catalans|website=www.bbc.com|date=23 September 2021}}</ref>

In October 2023, Hull KR confirmed that Johnson would depart the club following the expiry of his contract at the end of the season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=13 October 2023 |title=Hull KR confirm Luis Johnson departure amid further Craven Park exits |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-confirm-luis-johnson-8830865 |access-date=24 November 2023 |website=Hull Live}}</ref>

==== Castleford Tigers (loan) ====
In April 2023, Johnson re-joined his former academy club [[Castleford Tigers]] on a season-long loan.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=2023-04-02 |title=Castleford make loan signing from fellow Super League club |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/luis-johnson-castleford-hull-kr-loan |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> After two weeks, he would be available for recall by Hull KR in the event of significant injury problems.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-04-02 |title=Johnson Returns to the Fords |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8538 |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> Johnson made his Castleford debut on 6 April against [[Wakefield Trinity]] and went on to make a total of 3 appearances for the Tigers. In May, Hull KR exercised their recall clause and cut the loan short due to injury concerns throughout the squad.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=2023-05-16 |title=Hull KR recall Luis Johnson from Castleford loan spell |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-recall-luis-johnson-from-castleford-loan-spell |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-05-16 |title=Johnson recalled from loan |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8604 |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
On 18 December 2023, [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Johnson on a one-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=18 December 2023 |title=Luis Johnson: Castleford Tigers sign forward from Hull KR on one-year deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67754589 |access-date=18 December 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

==== Featherstone Rovers (loan) ====
On 13 June 2024, it was announced that Johnson had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] on a short-term loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=13 Jun 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Castleford Tigers forward Luis Johnson makes short-term loan move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-forward-luis-johnson-makes-short-term-loan-move/}}</ref>

===Doncaster RLFC===
On 22 July 2025, it was announced that he would join [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=22 Jul 2025|accessdate=31 Jul 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Castleford Tigers forward seals Championship deal following horror injury nightmare|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-castleford-tigers-forward-seals-championship-deal-following-horror-injury-nightmare}}</ref>
